---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 0ad287941ab60fca929f3c2d55f8cc1619ca3225
findings:
  - { id: judged-create-owners-value-shape, kind: judged, text: "Residual after the 0ad2879 fix: normalizeOwners closes the common paths (comma lists, bracketed literals, zero-name refusal by name), but an Owners value carrying a YAML-hostile character still reaches the strict-decode internal error the adjudicated finding said should never surface. An unbalanced bracket ('[alice') is not stripped and renders as the malformed literal '[[alice]'; a name containing a double quote or ': ' (e.g. 'O\"Brien' or 'role: alice') normalizes to a bracketed literal that safeScalar %q-quotes into a YAML STRING, so the owners: []string position fails DecodeSpec and the caller sees 'create scaffold failed self-validation: ...' rather than the named Owners-shape refusal. Fail-closed holds (nothing lands, TestBoardSpec_Create_OwnersNormalization pins the fixed paths), but the named-refusal guarantee is complete only for well-formed names — a narrow sliver of the original issue, disclosed rather than silently considered closed. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Disclosed residual: the named-refusal guarantee is complete for well-formed names; YAML-hostile names (unbalanced brackets, quotes, colons) fail closed with the generic self-validation error and nothing invalid ever lands. Completing the guarantee would require inventing an owner-name charset contract — a model-level question this story does not silently decide (provenance discipline); queued as a candidate model question, not a defect." }

integrity: sha256:44689bbd135789d9744deaaeafb0f513742ad5debb6ae013b5515d1b9ad72aa8

An unbalanced bracket ('[alice') is not stripped and renders as the malformed literal '[[alice]'; a name containing a double quote or ': ' (e.g. 'O\\\"Brien' or 'role: alice') normalizes to a bracketed literal that safeScalar %q-quotes into a YAML STRING, so the owners: []string position fails DecodeSpec and the caller sees 'create scaffold failed self-validation: ...' rather than the named Owners-shape refusal. Fail-closed holds (nothing lands, TestBoardSpec_Create_OwnersNormalization pins the fixed paths), but the named-refusal guarantee is complete only for well-formed names — a narrow sliver of the original issue, disclosed rather than silently considered closed.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/creation-form@0ad287941ab60fca929f3c2d55f8cc1619ca3225, spec/creation-form@04e6c0ff27a3644516b3aad6667c108c7c1af1b4], digest: sha256:38ae572c2fdab6ac79e8d11fd5471e97c920e3e6d02cf4913a5c08581ca47f6d, integrity: sha256:44689bbd135789d9744deaaeafb0f513742ad5debb6ae013b5515d1b9ad72aa8, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-create-owners-value-shape** [accepted-deviation]: Residual after the 0ad2879 fix: normalizeOwners closes the common paths (comma lists, bracketed literals, zero-name refusal by name), but an Owners value carrying a YAML-hostile character still reaches the strict-decode internal error the adjudicated finding said should never surface. An unbalanced bracket ('[alice') is not stripped and renders as the malformed literal '[[alice]'; a name containing a double quote or ': ' (e.g. 'O"Brien' or 'role: alice') normalizes to a bracketed literal that safeScalar %q-quotes into a YAML STRING, so the owners: []string position fails DecodeSpec and the caller sees 'create scaffold failed self-validation: ...' rather than the named Owners-shape refusal. Fail-closed holds (nothing lands, TestBoardSpec_Create_OwnersNormalization pins the fixed paths), but the named-refusal guarantee is complete only for well-formed names — a narrow sliver of the original issue, disclosed rather than silently considered closed. (confidence 0.20) — Disclosed residual: the named-refusal guarantee is complete for well-formed names; YAML-hostile names (unbalanced brackets, quotes, colons) fail closed with the generic self-validation error and nothing invalid ever lands. Completing the guarantee would require inventing an owner-name charset contract — a model-level question this story does not silently decide (provenance discipline); queued as a candidate model question, not a defect.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

(none)
